steps.title: Spot the task-difficulty confound
steps.content: The researchers judged monkeys by the same cognitive criteria even though some tasks required much more dexterity than others. That means performance could reflect physical difficulty instead of cognitive ability.

steps.title: State the flawed inference
steps.content: Choice B makes an unsupported distinction between tasks monkeys can and cannot perform. Choice C concerns other species, and Choice D concerns artificial versus wild problems. The remaining choice describes the misleading cognitive differences that the confound could create. The answer is A.
